steps.title: Translate the problem into variables
steps.content:
We have a 30-sided polygon with three possible side lengths. Let me identify what we're counting:
  • Let $n$ = number of sides with length 3 cm (this is what we're solving for)
  • The problem states the number of 8 cm sides is 5 times the number of 3 cm sides, so: number of 8 cm sides = $5n$
  • We're given: number of 4 cm sides = \$6$
  • Total sides in the polygon: 30

steps.title: Write the equation counting all sides
steps.content:
Since every side of the polygon must be one of the three lengths, the count of all sides must equal 30:

$$\text{(8 cm sides)} + \text{(3 cm sides)} + \text{(4 cm sides)} = 30$$

Substituting our expressions:

$$5n + n + 6 = 30$$

Combining like terms:

$$6n + 6 = 30$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
